hypothesis: A tiny Q0.24 score-comparator cutout is the cheapest next off-board validation gate for full-vocab rowwise top-k because it isolates the only new arithmetic and control contract introduced by rowwise sidecars: decode the low 24 scale bits, reject reserved metadata, multiply a signed accumulator by the unsigned scale, compare signed scaled scores, and apply the lower-token-id tie break without depending on model replay, DDR3, or board access.

cutout_contract.scope: single-row candidate acceptance and top1 update; no DDR3, no dot-product MACs, no full logits

decode.sidecar_endianness: little-endian word already presented as a 32-bit value
decode.reserved_policy: If any reserved bit is nonzero, assert out_error_reserved_bits and do not update the top1 state for that candidate.

score.overflow_policy: No saturation in the cutout; the 46-bit signed register is sized from the conservative bound in the fixed-point budget.

compare.tie_break: if scores are exactly equal, lower token id wins
compare.row_order_dependency: none; the tie break must make reversed candidate order deterministic
compare.initial_state: top score initialized below the minimum representable candidate score and top token initialized to all ones

pipeline.throughput_requirement: one accepted candidate every 4 cycles is sufficient for the planned 16-DSP full-vocab row interval, so a 2-cycle score unit can be overlapped
pipeline.allowed_first_cut: a simpler multi-cycle test-only implementation is acceptable if it preserves the externally visible compare contract

required_vectors.name: scaled_score_beats_raw_accumulator_order
required_vectors.expected: token 7 beats token 9 even though token 9 has the larger raw accumulator

required_vectors.name: signed_score_ordering
required_vectors.expected: token 4 wins over negative scaled scores

required_vectors.name: lower_token_tie_break
required_vectors.expected: token 3 wins an exact score tie with token 11 independent of arrival order

required_vectors.name: reserved_upper_byte_reject
required_vectors.expected: candidate with sidecar 0x01800000 is rejected and raises the reserved-bit error

required_vectors.name: conservative_register_bound
required_vectors.expected: positive 1048576 * 0x00ffffff fits and wins; negative counterpart is represented without overflow

pass: all named vectors match expected top token or expected decoder rejection
reserved upper bits never update top1 state
tie vector passes in both original and reversed candidate order
reported signed score for the bound vector equals 17592184995840
fail: raw accumulator order is used instead of scaled score order
reserved upper bits are silently accepted
exact score ties depend on row arrival order
score register truncates the conservative bound vector

validation.method: static cutout specification from checked-in artifacts plus sed readback only
validation.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

commands.cmd: sed reads of AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md and required Task 6 evidence artifacts

decision.rationale: The spec converts the handcomputed Q0.24 vectors into an implementation-ready RTL and testbench contract without starting model replay, synthesis, DDR3 integration, or any board action.
decision.next_gate: When Verilator and Python recover, implement the tiny comparator cutout and generate h2-q024-topk-comparator-cutout-result.json from the vector artifact.
